response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and web engineer with a broad background, but he fails to meet several hard requirements for this role. Most critically, he has no Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — native-level Japanese is a non-negotiable requirement for this position. Beyond that disqualifier, he has no demonstrated expertise in embedded networking, LAN-switch development, security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal), or Linux kernel development. His C experience is minimal and dated (NI LabWindows circa 2014), and his career has been firmly oriented toward web, TypeScript, and cloud-native development. The overlap between his profile and this role is too shallow to warrant an interview.
strengths: Over 15 years of software development experience across diverse stacks and industries
Some C/C++ exposure (National Instruments, embedded robotic arms at Altom) and general Linux familiarity
Demonstrated experience in embedded-adjacent work (hardware control, device orchestration tooling)
concerns: No Japanese language skills — native-level Japanese is a stated hard requirement and is completely absent from the resume
No experience in networking security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, L2/L3 switching, SNMP, NETCONF)
Primary expertise is web/TypeScript/Node.js; C proficiency is minimal and has not been the focus of his career for over a decade

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security domain experience
